title: California Opens $55 Million Incentive Program to Expand Public Electric Vehicle Fast Charging
content: The California Energy Commission (CEC) announced the launch of the Fast Charge California Project, a $55 million incentive program offering up to 100% of the installation costs for electric vehicle (EV) fast chargers at businesses and public sites across the state. Applications will be accepted through Oct. 29. The program is part of the California Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Project (CALeVIP), the nationâ€™s largest EV charging incentive initiative. Since 2017, CALeVIP has supported the deployment of nearly 10,000 EV chargers, helping to accommodate the stateâ€™s more than 2.2 million light-duty EVs, according to the Center for Sustainable Energy (CSE), the programâ€™s administrator.
author: Analysts Account
